name: arms overhead full sit-up (male)
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Extend your arms overhead, keeping them straight.
Step:3 Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is upright.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell bench front squat
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper chest, just below your collarbone.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, keeping your elbows up and your upper arms parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Lower your body down into a squat position by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell bench press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell bench squat
instructions: Step:1 Set up a barbell on a squat rack at chest height.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the rack, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Lift the barbell off the rack and step back, ensuring your feet are still shoulder-width apart.
Step:6 Lower your body down into a squat, keeping your knees in line with your toes.
Step:7 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell bent over row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips while keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your lower chest by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell biceps curl (with arm blaster)
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:2 Place your upper arms against the arm blaster, keeping your elbows close to your torso.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Biceps Curl Avec Bras Blaster)

name: barbell clean and press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the floor in front of you.
Step:2 Bend your knees and hinge at the hips to lower down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor, keeping it close to your body.
Step:4 As the barbell reaches your thighs, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the barbell up towards your chest.
Step:5 As the barbell reaches chest height, quickly drop under it and catch it at shoulder level, with your elbows pointing forward and your palms facing up.
Step:6 From the catch position, press the barbell overhead by extending your arms and pushing the barbell straight up.
Step:7 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell clean-grip front squat
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper chest, with your elbows pointing forward.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, as if you are sitting back into a chair.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and your back straight as you lower down, making sure your knees do not go past your toes.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing forward.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your torso and exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:3 Continue to raise the bar until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale as you slowly begin to lower the bar back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the ground in front of you.
Step:2 Bend your knees and hinge at the hips to lower your torso and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and chest lifted as you drive through your heels to lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees.
Step:4 As you stand up straight, squeeze your glutes and keep your core engaged.
Step:5 Lower the barbell back down to the ground by bending at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell decline bench press
instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell decline wide-grip press
instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows out to the sides.
Step:4 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell drag curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the barbell up towards your chest by contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell floor calf raise
instructions: Step:1 Place a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Step:2 Stand with the balls of your feet on the edge of the barbell, with your heels hanging off.
Step:3 Hold onto a stable object for balance if needed.
Step:4 Raise your heels as high as possible, using your calves to lift your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell front chest squat
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell in front of your chest with your hands shoulder-width apart, elbows pointing forward.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell front raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and lift the barbell forward and upward until it reaches shoulder level.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell front raise and pullover
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and raise the barbell in front of you until it reaches shoulder height.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Next, lower the barbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell front squat
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell in front of your shoulders, resting it on your collarbone and shoulders.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell full squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Step:4 Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Step:5 Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:6 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:7 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell full squat (back pov)
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Step:4 Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Step:5 Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:6 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:7 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell full squat (side pov)
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Step:4 Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Step:5 Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:6 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:7 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell full zercher squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell in the crooks of your elbows, with your hands gripping the barbell for stability.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you lower your hips back and down into a squat position.
Step:4 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:5 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:6 Drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell guillotine bench press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell slowly towards your neck, keeping your elbows pointed outwards.
Step:4 Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your neck.
Step:5 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell hack squat
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell behind your legs, resting it on your upper thighs.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell high bar squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Place the barbell on your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to squat down, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:4 Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell incline bench press
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell incline close grip bench press
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Incliné Prise serrée Prise Couché Développé

name: barbell incline reverse-grip press
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie back on the bench and grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Unrack the barbell and lower it towards your upper chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell incline row
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie face down on the bench with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell jefferson squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, resting it on the front of your body, just below your waist.
Step:3 Step your left foot forward and your right foot back, keeping your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:5 Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat the movement, alternating your forward and back foot with each repetition.

name: barbell jm bench press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Step:4 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell lateral lunge
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Step:3 Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your left leg straight.
Step:4 Push off with your right foot and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side, stepping with your left foot.

name: barbell low bar squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper back.
Step:2 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, slowly lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell lunge
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your right knee until your thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell lying extension
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell lying preacher curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and your arms extended over the edge, holding a barbell with an underhand grip.
Step:2 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:3 Continue to raise the bar until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell lying triceps extension
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell lying triceps extension skull crusher
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your forehead, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Allongé Triceps Extension Skull Crusher

name: barbell narrow stance squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you slowly lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell one arm bent over row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a barbell with one hand using an overhand grip.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your head in a neutral position.
Step:3 Pull the barbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
nameFr: Barre Un Bras Buste penché Penché Rowing

name: barbell one arm floor press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with one hand, palm facing up, and extend your arm straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
nameFr: Barre Un Bras Au sol Développé

name: barbell one arm side deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Bend at the hips and lower the barbell towards the outside of your leg, keeping your arm straight and your chest up.
Step:4 Lower the barbell as far as you can while maintaining good form.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
nameFr: Barre Un Bras Side Soulevé de terre

name: barbell overhead squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with a wide grip, positioning it overhead with your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Au-dessus de la tête Squat

name: barbell pendlay row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell preacher curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a preacher bench with your upper arms resting on the pad and your chest against the support.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an underhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell press sit-up
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back on a mat with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, resting it on your chest.
Step:3 Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell prone incline curl
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting against it.
Step:3 Hold a barbell with an underhand grip, shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Extend your arms fully, allowing the barbell to hang down towards the floor.
Step:5 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:6 Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Step:7 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:8 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:9 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell pullover to press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, lower the barbell behind your head in an arc-like motion until you feel a stretch in your lats.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then reverse the motion and press the barbell back to the starting position above your chest.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell rack pull
instructions: Step:1 Set up a barbell on a rack at knee height.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards.
Step:3 Bend at the hips and knees to lower yourself down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Engage your core and lift the barbell by extending your hips and knees, pulling your shoulders back and squeezing your glutes at the top.
Step:5 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position by bending at the hips and knees.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell rear delt row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell revers wrist curl v. 2
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:4 Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upward as far as possible.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell reverse curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Keep your upper arms stationary and exhale as you curl the barbell upward, contracting your biceps.
Step:3 Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale as you slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell reverse grip bent over row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, until your torso is almost parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Prise inversée Prise Buste penché Penché Rowing

name: barbell reverse grip decline bench press
instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Prise inversée Prise Décliné Couché Développé

name: barbell reverse preacher curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and your arms extended straight down, holding a barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell upward while contracting your biceps.
Step:3 Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell reverse wrist curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell rollerout from bench
instructions: Step:1 Start by kneeling on the floor with a barbell placed on a bench in front of you.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, slowly roll the barbell forward, extending your arms in front of you.
Step:4 Continue rolling the barbell forward until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly roll the barbell back towards your body, returning to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell romanian deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend at the hips, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping it close to your body.
Step:5 Feel the stretch in your hamstrings as you lower the barbell.
Step:6 Once you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, push your hips forward and stand up straight.
Step:7 Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:8 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell seated behind head military press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and bring it down to shoulder level, behind your head.
Step:4 Press the barbell upward until your arms are fully extended.
Step:5 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Assis Behind Head Militaire Développé

name: barbell seated bradford rocky press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent and pointing forward.
Step:4 Press the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Lower the barbell back to shoulder height and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell seated calf raise
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and a barbell resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Place the balls of your feet on a raised platform, such as a block or step.
Step:3 Lower your heels as far as possible, feeling a stretch in your calves.
Step:4 Raise your heels as high as possible, contracting your calves.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell seated calf raise
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and a barbell resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Place the balls of your feet on a raised platform, such as a block or step.
Step:3 Position the barbell across your thighs and hold it securely with your hands.
Step:4 Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, lift your heels off the ground by extending your ankles.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell seated overhead press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and bring it to shoulder level, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Step:4 Press the barbell overhead by extending your arms fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to shoulder level.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Assis Au-dessus de la tête Développé

name: barbell seated overhead triceps extension
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and raise it overhead.
Step:3 Lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms close to your head.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms to raise the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Assis Au-dessus de la tête Triceps Extension

name: barbell side split squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, bending at the knees and hips.
Step:4 As you lower, push your knees out to the sides and keep your weight on your heels.
Step:5 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell side split squat v. 2
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards.
Step:2 Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your shoulders.
Step:3 Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Step:4 Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Step:5 Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat on the other side, stepping out with your left foot.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell speed squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your hips back and down, as if sitting into a chair.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell split squat v. 2
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Take a large step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your knees and hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg forward for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell standing back wrist curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Rest the barbell on the back of your hands with your palms facing down and your fingers pointing towards your body.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upwards as far as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell standing bradford press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell in front of your shoulders with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Press the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:3 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell standing calf raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using only your toes.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell standing close grip curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, hands close together.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Step:3 Exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps. Continue to raise the bar until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale as you slowly begin to bring the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Prise serrée Prise Curl

name: barbell standing close grip military press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly closer than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Press the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Step:4 Lower the barbell back to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Prise serrée Prise Militaire Développé

name: barbell standing concentration curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in one hand, palm facing up.
Step:2 Rest your opposite hand on your thigh for support.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the weight up towards your shoulder.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the weight back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: barbell standing front raise over head
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:3 Slowly raise the barbell in front of you, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Step:4 Continue lifting until the barbell is slightly above shoulder level.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Avant Élévation Penché Head

name: barbell standing leg calf raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using your calves.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell standing overhead triceps extension
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Raise the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Au-dessus de la tête Triceps Extension

name: barbell standing reverse grip curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Step:3 Exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps, bringing the barbell as close to your shoulders as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Prise inversée Prise Curl

name: barbell standing rocking leg calf raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, balancing on the balls of your feet.
Step:3 Slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Rocking Jambe Mollet Élévation

name: barbell standing wide grip biceps curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Step:3 Exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Prise large Prise Biceps Curl

name: barbell standing wide military press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Step:2 Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly in front of the bar.
Step:3 Press the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Step:4 Lower the barbell back to shoulder height and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Debout Prise large Militaire Développé

name: barbell standing wide-grip curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palms facing away from your body.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell upward by contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell straight leg deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend at your hips and lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Lower the barbell until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:5 Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Jambes tendues Jambe Soulevé de terre

name: barbell sumo deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outwards.
Step:2 Place a barbell on the ground in front of you, centered between your feet.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, to grip the barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:4 Engage your core and drive through your heels to lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
Step:5 As you lift, keep your chest up and back straight, and push your hips forward to fully engage your glutes.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell upright row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight and core engaged, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell upright row v. 2
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell upright row v. 3
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your core engaged and back straight, exhale as you lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell wide bench press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows slightly flared out.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell wide reverse grip bench press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a wide reverse grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in and your wrists straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Barre Prise large Prise inversée Prise Couché Développé

name: barbell wide squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell wide-grip upright row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell wrist curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your forearms resting on your thighs, holding a barbell with an underhand grip.
Step:2 Allow the barbell to roll down to your fingertips, keeping your wrists straight.
Step:3 Slowly curl the barbell up towards your forearms by flexing your wrists.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell wrist curl v. 2
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:4 Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your forearms.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell zercher squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell in the crooks of your elbows, with your hands gripping the bar for stability.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you lower your hips back and down into a squat position.
Step:4 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bench dip (knees bent)
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench and straighten your legs in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bench dip on floor
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bench hip extension
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back against the bench and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands on the bench for support.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and hamstrings, then lift your hips off the bench until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bench pull-ups
instructions: Step:1 Position yourself under a bar or a sturdy horizontal surface that is at chest height.
Step:2 Grab the bar or surface with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hang with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Step:4 Pull your chest towards the bar or surface by squeezing your shoulder blades together and bending your elbows.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar or surface.
Step:6 Lower yourself back down to the starting position with control.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: biceps leg concentration curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your legs spread apart and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Step:3 With your palm facing up, curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:4 Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: biceps narrow pull-ups
instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing towards you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull yourself up towards the bar, focusing on using your biceps to lift your body.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: biceps pull-up
instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull yourself up by bending your elbows, bringing your chest towards the bar.
Step:3 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bodyweight incline side plank
instructions: Step:1 Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Step:2 Place your forearm on the ground directly below your shoulder, with your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your feet.
Step:4 Hold this position for the desired amount of time.
Step:5 Lower your hips back down to the ground and repeat on the other side.

name: bodyweight side lying biceps curl
instructions: Step:1 Lie on your side with your legs extended and your head supported by your arm.
Step:2 Hold your upper arm against your side and bend your elbow to curl your forearm towards your shoulder.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your forearm back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bodyweight squatting row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding onto a sturdy object or suspension trainer with your arms extended.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your knees behind your toes.
Step:3 From the squat position, pull your body up towards the object or suspension trainer, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bodyweight squatting row (with towel)
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a towel in front of you with your palms facing down.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:3 As you lower into the squat, simultaneously pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then slowly return to the starting position while extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Poids du corps Squatting Rowing Avec Towel)

name: bodyweight standing calf raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Place your hands on a wall or stable surface for balance.
Step:3 Slowly raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bodyweight standing one arm row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Let the dumbbell hang straight down in front of you, with your arm fully extended.
Step:4 Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:5 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Step:6 Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
nameFr: Poids du corps Debout Un Bras Rowing

name: bodyweight standing row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp a bar or handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the bar or handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bodyweight standing row (with towel)
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel in front of you with both hands.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Poids du corps Debout Rowing Avec Towel)

name: cable bar lateral pulldown
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach a straight bar.
Step:2 Sit facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lean back slightly and keep your chest up, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Step:5 Pull the bar down towards your chest, leading with your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable bench press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step forward to create tension in the cables.
Step:4 Position your feet firmly on the ground and engage your core.
Step:5 Bend your elbows and bring your hands to shoulder level, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:6 Push the handles forward, extending your arms fully in front of you.
Step:7 Pause for a moment, then slowly reverse the movement, bringing your hands back to shoulder level.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable close grip curl
instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Exhale and curl the bar up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Step:7 Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable concentration curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip and rest your elbow against the inside of your thigh.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the cable handle towards your shoulder while contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then inhale and slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: cable cross-over lateral pulldown
instructions: Step:1 Attach a cable handle to each side of a cable machine at shoulder height.
Step:2 Stand in the middle of the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step back to create tension in the cables.
Step:4 Lean forward slightly from the hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:5 Pull the handles down and across your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable cross-over revers fly
instructions: Step:1 Attach a D-handle to each low pulley cable and stand in the middle of the cable crossover machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with a pronated grip (palms facing down) and take a step forward, positioning your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and lean forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your abs engaged.
Step:4 With your arms extended out to the sides and slightly bent at the elbows, exhale and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the cables back and upward in a reverse fly motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:3 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Step:4 Exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend at the hips and knees, lowering your torso until your back is parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your shoulders back.
Step:4 Engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift the cable handles, extending your hips and standing up straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handles back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable decline fly
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the handles with your palms facing forward and your arms extended straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable decline one arm press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine and grab the handle with one hand.
Step:3 Position yourself with your back against the decline bench and your arm extended straight in front of you.
Step:4 Bend your elbow and lower the handle towards your chest while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the handle back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: cable decline press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position them at chest level.
Step:4 Keep your feet flat on the ground and your back firmly against the bench.
Step:5 Exhale and push the handles away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, squeezing your chest muscles.
Step:7 Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable decline seated wide-grip row
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine with your feet securely placed on the footrests.
Step:2 Grasp the cable attachment with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the cable towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the cable back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable drag curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable floor seated wide-grip row
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your back straight.
Step:2 Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and position the cable machine behind you.
Step:3 Grasp the handle with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:4 Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your chest lifted.
Step:5 Pull the handle towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Poulie Au sol Assis Wide-grip Rowing

name: cable front raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Raise the cable handle in front of you, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Step:4 Continue lifting until your arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable hammer curl (with rope)
instructions: Step:1 Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Step:2 Hold the cable rope attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing each other, and your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Continue to raise the cable rope attachment until your biceps are fully contracted and the rope is at shoulder level.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the cable rope attachment back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable high pulley overhead tricep extension
instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a high pulley and stand facing away from the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the rope with both hands and extend your arms overhead.
Step:3 Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary.
Step:4 Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Poulie Haut Pulley Au-dessus de la tête Triceps Extension

name: cable incline bench press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the bench to a 45-degree incline.
Step:2 Attach the cable handles to the high pulleys.
Step:3 Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:4 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and bring them to shoulder height.
Step:5 Push the handles forward and upward until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the handles back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable incline bench row
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable handle to the low pulley.
Step:2 Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip and extend your arms fully in front of you.
Step:4 Lean forward from your hips while keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the cable handle towards your chest by retracting your shoulder blades and bending your elbows.
Step:6 Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement, then slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable incline fly
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
Step:2 Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable incline triceps extension
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low pulley position.
Step:2 Attach a straight bar to the cable.
Step:3 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight overhead.
Step:5 Lean forward slightly, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Step:6 Bend your elbows and lower the bar behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Step:7 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable lateral pulldown (with rope attachment)
instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope attachment to the cable machine at a high position.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and lean slightly back.
Step:5 Pull the rope down towards your sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the tension and allow the rope to return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Poulie Latéral Tirage vertical Avec Corde Attachment)

name: cable lateral pulldown with v-bar
instructions: Step:1 Sit down on the cable pulldown machine and grab the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Adjust the knee pad so that your thighs are secured under it.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean back slightly.
Step:4 Pull the v-bar down towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Squeeze your back muscles at the bottom of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly return the v-bar to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
nameFr: Poulie Latéral Tirage vertical Avec Barre v

name: cable lateral raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable low fly
instructions: Step:1 Attach the handles to the low pulleys of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Step:2 Stand in the middle of the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Maintaining control, slowly bring your arms forward in a sweeping motion, crossing them in front of your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, feeling the stretch in your chest muscles.
Step:6 Reverse the motion and slowly return your arms to the starting position, keeping tension on your chest muscles throughout.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable low seated row
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward, maintaining a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable lying bicep curl
instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Lie face up on a flat bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:5 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the bar up towards your shoulders.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Step:7 Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable lying fly
instructions: Step:1 Attach the handles to the cables and lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the handles with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight above your chest.
Step:3 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring your arms back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable lying triceps extension v. 2
instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Lie down on a flat bench facing up, with your head towards the cable machine.
Step:3 Grasp the rope handle with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:4 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the rope handle towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable middle fly
instructions: Step:1 Attach cables to both sides of a cable machine at chest height.
Step:2 Stand in the center of the machine with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides.
Step:4 Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a slight forward lean.
